title: Conic Discriminant PASS: 4 hyperbola + 4 ellipse witnesses + convergent sequence
_fixture_purpose: General theorem witnesses. For any QA direction (d,e): I=C-F=2de-(d²-e²) determines conic type. The silver-ratio continued fraction convergents 2/1, 5/2, 12/5, 29/12, 70/29 alternate hyperbola/ellipse with |I|=1 at each step — the closest integer approach to the parabola boundary. The QA elements G=F+C+I-I=F+C and I are related by G²=F²+C² (cert [125]); sign(I) is independent of G. Chromogeometry: I=Qg(d,e)-Qr(d,e)=C-F; negative means red dominates (more 'stretch'), positive means green dominates (more 'rotation').

general_theorem.type_rule: I>0 ↔ hyperbola; I=0 ↔ parabola (impossible for integers); I<0 ↔ ellipse
general_theorem.threshold: d/e = 1+√2 (silver ratio) separates hyperbola from ellipse
general_theorem.independence: sign(I) independent of scale; if (d,e)→(kd,ke) then I→k²(C-F) same sign
general_theorem.chromogeometry: I = Qg-Qr = green minus red quadrance; sign = dominance of one color metric

hyperbola_witnesses._note: Plimpton 322 Row 1 — barely hyperbolic; d/e=2.4 just below silver ratio 2.414

ellipse_witnesses._note: Closest ellipse to parabola boundary; d/e=2.5 just above silver ratio
Convergent 29/12 — alternating with Row 1 (12,5); |I|=1, barely elliptic

convergent_sequence._note: Silver ratio 1+√2=[2;2,2,2,...]; convergents alternate H(I=1)/E(I=-1) with each convergent one step closer to parabola
convergent_sequence.pattern: |I|=1 at every convergent; type alternates H/E; d/e→silver ratio from both sides
